narrative: Two-act demo. Act 1 — existential / IP: a frontier-lab API key in a repo and confidential Atlas Labs / Project Meridian eval rubric in Slack, either of which breaches the NDA-bound trust the AI business runs on. Act 2 — PII at scale: contractor payout TIN/bank/routing and a Snowflake export of student PII (FERPA). Then a precision sweep over internal IDs / hashes / placeholder keys to tell the no-alert-fatigue story.

findings.finding: Handshake AI is an expert network that supplies human model-validation (prompts, rubrics, output evaluation) to frontier AI labs via a proprietary annotation platform; expert feedback is piped into labs' training pipelines through APIs.
500k+ PhDs / 3M+ grad scholars across ~200 specialties, sourced from the legacy career network of 18M students/alumni across 1,500 universities.
Experts are paid roughly $40–$125/hr as contractors — implying 1099/W-9 tax data (TIN/SSN) and bank/routing payout details on file.
The AI unit scaled 15 → 150 staff in 8 months; $0 → $50M ARR in 4 months, $100M+ projected first year. Hyper-growth = many new hires and elevated leak surface.
Acquired Cleanlab (data-quality ML) for talent + tech — implies a strong ML/eng org and dataset/content hashes that look like secrets.

Confidential frontier-lab project data (eval rubrics, prompts, held-out benchmarks) under NDA is the highest-stakes asset; regime spans FERPA (students), GDPR/CCPA (consumer PII), SOC 2 (selling to labs), IRS 1099/W-9, and contractual confidentiality.
findings.source: inference from business model + regulated data types

hypotheses.hypothesis: The external annotator/expert workforce coordinates in Slack/Discord channels, and those channels should be monitored for sensitive info (confidential lab task content, PII) pasted in chat.
hypotheses.basis: Defining workforce is 500k+ external experts working confidential lab data through a platform — they coordinate somewhere, and that chat is the least-obvious high-value surface.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Red-team / adversarial / synthetic training content (harmful prompts, jailbreak strings, synthetic PII used as examples) must NOT be flagged — it is legitimate work product, not a leak.
hypotheses.basis: The Handshake AI blog states they exclude harmful/abusive content, which implies they routinely handle adversarial content as a category; their deliverable looks like a violation by design.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Frontier-lab egress keys / API tokens leaking out of the eval pipeline (repo, CI, Slack) would compromise a customer's account and breach the NDA.
hypotheses.basis: Expert feedback is piped into labs via APIs → live customer keys exist in their pipeline.

hypotheses.hypothesis: FERPA-protected student PII from the legacy career network could co-mingle into AI-side exports/warehouse.
hypotheses.basis: 18M-student career network and the AI data business sit under one roof.

decisions.rationale: User directive, and it maps to the existential risk: leaking a lab's API key or eval rubric breaks the NDA trust the $100M business depends on.
decisions.drove: lab/AWS keys across .env, settings.py, Slack, CI log, service-account, email; confidential rubric + NDA carriers

decisions.rationale: User directive for a hands-on POC environment.
decisions.drove: 13 carriers across 9 surfaces; every CSV row indexed

decisions.choice: GitHub, Slack, Google Drive, Snowflake, Annotation Platform, Email, Salesforce, CI, Zendesk
decisions.rationale: Inferred modern SF AI-company stack plus their own annotation platform (product) and a CX surface; each becomes a carrier in its native format.

decisions.choice: Engineering, ML Infra (ex-Cleanlab), AI Training Ops, Expert Network Ops, Payments, Trust & Safety, Partnerships, HR
decisions.rationale: From the business model + Cleanlab acquisition — grounds who-leaks-what so the data reads as one company's sprawl.
decisions.drove: believable persona attribution on every planted item

decisions.choice: secret, confidential, PII, financial (no PHI/PCI as Handshake-held)
decisions.rationale: Two data worlds: secrets/IP, and contractor/student PII + payout banking. PHI appears only as RLHF *content*, not Handshake's own records; no central card processing → PCI out of scope.
decisions.drove: category mix; medical detail confined to an expert answer, not a patient record

decisions.choice: expert/student IDs shaped like SSNs, placeholder example keys, UUID/git-SHA/content hashes, PAN-shaped opportunity numbers
decisions.rationale: A 18M-user marketplace + Cleanlab data-quality work is saturated with ID/hash strings that look sensitive — the precision / no-alert-fatigue story.

decisions.choice: base64/line-split lab keys, spaced TIN, PII in expert free-text, spelled-out bank account
decisions.rationale: How sensitive data realistically shows up in forms a whitespace-fragile detector misses — the recall story.
Avoid implying a real customer relationship while keeping the scenario realistic.
decisions.drove: consistent customer/codename across env, Slack, JSON, CRM, email

decisions.choice: exercise SSN across 3 surfaces; index every CSV row
decisions.rationale: Earlier review found per-type context coverage was thin — broadened framings/surfaces so the demo tests detection, not grep.
decisions.drove: SSN in Drive cell + email prose + Zendesk ticket; ~99 indexed values

open_questions: Provider-key mix (AWS/GCP/Stripe/GitHub/Slack/OpenAI) is assumed — their actual stack would refine which keys to plant.
Document carriers (.xlsx offer letter, signed NDA .pdf) deferred — text carriers only for now.
Confirm the fabricated 'Atlas Labs' / 'Project Meridian' naming is acceptable, or swap to your own.
PHI intentionally excluded as a Handshake-held category (appears only as RLHF content) — confirm that's right for the demo.
